title: Discovery Engine — eight-stage cross-disciplinary research-question discovery apparatus
public_claim: A local research-development application with two linked halves: cross-field mechanism discovery and dependency-aware academic workflow.
distinctive_mechanism: The discovery side searches for transferable mechanisms from other fields and tests whether they survive context, language, timing, and analogy limits; Observatory, Scanner, Bridge, Cross-Pollination Lab, Assay Lab, Crucible, Incubator, Forge, and the Galaxy Map make those cross-field mechanism relations visible. The academic side carries problem exploration, problem statement, literature review, methodology, analysis, discussion, conclusion, and editing through stage rooms with instruments and standards; upstream changes can mark downstream stages stale, and downstream revisions can force.
use_value: This is the distinctive Nexus claim: it is both an originality engine and a dependency-governed research pipeline, helping a researcher generate non-obvious ideas while keeping the eventual paper aligned from problem statement through final argument.

flagship_proof_builds.name: Cross-field mechanism transfer demonstration
flagship_proof_builds.claim: Nexus is not ordinary keyword search; it is designed to find transferable mechanisms across fields and route them into testable research objects.
flagship_proof_builds.input: one seed research problem, at least two source fields, and candidate mechanism families
flagship_proof_builds.operation: use the Galaxy Map / Cross-Field Mechanism Atlas, Bridge mapping, and XPol fit pressure to move from source-field pattern to target-field research question
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: transfer route, fit-pressure record, rejected false analogy, and draftable research object
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: show one initial research question entering Observatory/Galaxy, one Bridge mapping, one XPol pressure test, and one Forge-ready output with stale-state rules visible

flagship_proof_builds.name: Upstream/downstream stale-state demonstration
flagship_proof_builds.claim: The academic pipeline is wired so changes to problem statement, method, evidence, or analysis force downstream review instead of silently leaving later prose stale.
flagship_proof_builds.input: a project with at least problem statement, literature review, methodology, analysis, and draft stages
flagship_proof_builds.operation: change one upstream stage and verify that dependent stages become review-required
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: dependency warning, stale-stage flags, and required revalidation tasks
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: alter one upstream decision and capture the downstream stale-state propagation path

flagship_proof_builds.name: Forge provenance and release gate demonstration
flagship_proof_builds.claim: Nexus can convert mature findings into shareable writing only when section support and claim ceilings are visible.
flagship_proof_builds.input: one mature finding, draft lane, support sidecar, source IDs, and release matrix
flagship_proof_builds.operation: move the finding through Forge Intake, Draft, Lineage, and Release Brake
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: draft section with provenance, export permission or export block, and public claim boundary
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: run one draft through Forge and show whether export is allowed, blocked, or narrowed

component_proofs.claim_supported: renders the source and idea terrain as discipline clusters, local neighborhoods, transfer arcs, and overlay layers so the researcher can look for non-obvious mechanism transfer rather than only keyword relevance
component_proofs.input: disciplines, subfields, candidate papers, scan results, field-status metrics, and candidate transfer arcs
component_proofs.expected_output: candidate research neighborhoods, cross-field routes, gap surfaces, and transfer candidates for Bridge and XPol testing
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (disciplines, subfields, candidate papers, scan...) produces output (candidate research neighborhoods, cross-field routes...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.component: Upstream / Downstream Dependency And Stale-State Atlas
component_proofs.claim_supported: marks later research stages as needing review when an upstream problem statement, method rule, source basis, or analytical result changes
component_proofs.input: academic stages, room dependency graph, upstream changes, downstream surfaces, stale counts, and blast-radius relationships
component_proofs.expected_output: dependency warnings, stale-stage flags, blast-radius cues, and revalidation tasks
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (academic stages, room dependency graph, upstream...) produces output (dependency warnings, stale-stage flags, blast-radius...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: locates the project in a consequential problem space before the thesis hardens into a premature claim
component_proofs.input: field scan, gap hypothesis, research stake, motive check, and early falsifier risk
component_proofs.expected_output: frontier note naming field, gap pressure, motive for inquiry, and early falsifier risk
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Problem Exploration / Frontier Pressure Map; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: converts the explored terrain into a bounded research problem whose question can be answered, challenged, or revised
component_proofs.input: alignment, core claim or question, question quality, reasoning flow, scope boundary, falsifiers, and significance
component_proofs.expected_output: problem statement with scope boundary, question form, stakes, assumptions, and re-check triggers
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Problem Statement / Formulation Instrument; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: turns the literature review from a bibliography into a map of canon, disputes, gaps, and the project's intervention
component_proofs.input: search strategy, canonical sources, schools of thought, unresolved debates, critical synthesis, review organization, and project position
component_proofs.expected_output: literature-position artifact distinguishing canon, disagreement, gap logic, and contribution boundary
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Literature Review / Scholarship Terrain Board; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: tests whether the design, evidence plan, validation logic, and limitation ceiling fit the research question before results or claims move downstream
component_proofs.input: research design blueprint, methodology-method-design distinction, method choice, method rationale, data/evidence, qualitative/quantitative fit, reporting plan, integration.
component_proofs.expected_output: method blueprint with design rationale, evidence role, validation checks, integration logic, and explicit claim limits
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Methodology / Design-Fit Tribunal; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: records what the evidence says before discussion explains what it means, preserving contradictions and display fit
component_proofs.input: outcome convention, results or findings, description/interpretation boundary, evidence display plan, contradictions, alternative explanations, null/unexpected results, and.
component_proofs.expected_output: findings ledger separating observed result, display plan, analytic pattern, contradiction, and interpretive boundary
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Analysis / Results-Findings Boundary Board; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: interprets findings against the literature and method without importing new results or letting significance outrun support
component_proofs.input: salient outcomes, interpretation, contribution, implications, limits and validity, alternatives/unexpected findings, causal language and hedging, and focus control
component_proofs.expected_output: discussion frame with implications, alternatives, limitations, and relationship back to the research question
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Discussion / Interpretation And Significance Prism; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: closes the argument at the highest responsible claim level while preserving retraction conditions and next-study paths
component_proofs.input: what we now know, claim level C1-C5, further research, and what would change the conclusion
component_proofs.expected_output: bounded conclusion with contribution, claim boundary, unresolved uncertainty, and next-study agenda
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Conclusion / Claim-Ceiling Ladder; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: checks whether each stage has the required scholarly burden and whether visually polished material is blocked until direct artifacts are present
component_proofs.input: stage requirement IDs, professor research process sources, direct-proof status, and presentation-readiness contracts
component_proofs.expected_output: requirement-status map and presentation-readiness warning for the active academic stage
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Academic Requirement Atlas / Professor-Facing Gate; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: shows how weak or changed upstream stages make later parts of the scholarly argument chain require review
component_proofs.input: academic pipeline nodes, stage state, artifact contracts, stale flags, and upstream_of relationships
component_proofs.expected_output: dependency-aware scholarly argument spine with stale-stage warnings
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Academic Helix / Downstream Staleness Map; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: binds one mature finding to one output template before a drafting lane can open
component_proofs.input: mature upstream finding, selected release template, existing drafts, and readiness condition
component_proofs.expected_output: new draft lane or blocked intake state
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Forge Intake / Draft Lane Opener; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: revises one draft while keeping support, evidence boundary, and export consequences visible beside the prose
component_proofs.input: selected draft sections, editable content, support sidecar, template fields, and provenance notes
component_proofs.expected_output: stronger supported draft with section-level revision state
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Forge Draft / Revision Desk; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: tests whether each section carries enough provenance to be treated as releasable writing
component_proofs.input: draft section, claim IDs, evidence IDs, source IDs, unknown support IDs, sentence counts, and support blockers
component_proofs.expected_output: section-support decision, claim ceiling, and export-allowed or export-blocked state
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Forge Lineage / Section Support Atlas; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: decides whether a draft is ready to leave the lab and what must remain visible when it does
component_proofs.input: draft readiness, release matrix, quality score, status, provenance audit, and portfolio consequence
component_proofs.expected_output: release-ready export decision or explicit release block
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Forge Release / Export Decision Board; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: blocks export when draft prose outruns section support or when the reader cannot see the source trail
component_proofs.input: substantive section count, unsupported section count, mapped sentence count, estimated sentence count, export_allowed flag, and claim ceiling
component_proofs.expected_output: completion/export permission and public claim boundary
component_proofs.verification_check: Move one project through Forge Release Brake / Claim-Ceiling Gate; confirm stage output is recorded and downstream stale-state or release gates update.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to see which fields remain disconnected or weakly bridged
component_proofs.input: discipline topology, transfer edges, and gap signals
component_proofs.expected_output: candidate bridge zones and under-connected terrain signals
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (discipline topology, transfer edges, and gap signals) produces output (candidate bridge zones and under-connected terrain...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need shared terms, datasets, or artifacts that can support cross-field coordination
component_proofs.input: artifact links, mapped candidates, and adjacent discipline records
component_proofs.expected_output: candidate bridge artifacts worth sending to Find Sources, Evaluate Sources, or Map Cross-Field Links
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (artifact links, mapped candidates, and adjacent...) produces output (candidate bridge artifacts worth sending to Find...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to know which fields can actually receive transferred concepts or methods
component_proofs.input: discipline maturity, related work, institutional readiness, and prior mappings
component_proofs.expected_output: capacity estimates and likely recipient fields
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (discipline maturity, related work, institutional...) produces output (capacity estimates and likely recipient fields) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to balance frontier scanning against deeper development of known terrain
component_proofs.input: gap yield, tractability, novelty, and queue pressure
component_proofs.expected_output: recommended allocation between exploration and consolidation
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (gap yield, tractability, novelty, and queue pressure) produces output (recommended allocation between exploration...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you want weak but intriguing cross-domain signals that deserve analyst review
component_proofs.input: adjacency anomalies, transfer hints, and ambient field activity
component_proofs.expected_output: candidate leads for exploratory scanning or mapping
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (adjacency anomalies, transfer hints, and ambient field...) produces output (candidate leads for exploratory scanning or mapping) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need the temporal sequence of scans, evaluations, mappings, and outputs
component_proofs.input: pipeline events, evaluation history, and output timestamps
component_proofs.expected_output: stage chronology and momentum readouts
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (pipeline events, evaluation history, and output...) produces output (stage chronology and momentum readouts) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to decide which field, term set, or adjacency lane to search next
component_proofs.input: gap register, observatory priorities, and current corpus coverage
component_proofs.expected_output: recommended retrieval directions and search refinements
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (gap register, observatory priorities, and current...) produces output (recommended retrieval directions and search refinements) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to see whether the current scan is repeating literature already represented
component_proofs.input: corpus inventory, prior scans, and source fingerprints
component_proofs.expected_output: duplication warnings and retrieval-pruning suggestions
component_proofs.verification_check: Replay one clean/poisoned case through Coverage Overlap; confirm exposure, uptake or rejection, reviewer burden, and output: duplication warnings and retrieval-pruning suggestions.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you want to know which lanes are most likely to produce usable candidates
component_proofs.input: discipline history, gap profile, and recent retrieval outcomes
component_proofs.expected_output: expected candidate yield by field or search lane
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (discipline history, gap profile, and recent retrieval...) produces output (expected candidate yield by field or search lane) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to keep retrieval effort proportional to likely analytical value
component_proofs.input: queue size, scan breadth, and retrieval cost signals
component_proofs.expected_output: budget warnings and scale recommendations
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (queue size, scan breadth, and retrieval cost signals) produces output (budget warnings and scale recommendations) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need larger multi-field retrieval after a narrow search has clarified direction
component_proofs.input: approved search lanes, queue settings, and corpus targets
component_proofs.expected_output: queued broad retrieval runs and expanded candidate sets
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Batch Retrieval; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: queued broad retrieval runs and expanded candidate sets.

component_proofs.claim_supported: a source is known or recommended but would not be captured by automated retrieval
component_proofs.input: manual references, external leads, and analyst-selected items
component_proofs.expected_output: governed additions to the corpus
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (manual references, external leads...) produces output (governed additions to the corpus) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need the full claims-and-support record behind a candidate
component_proofs.input: candidate notes, extracted claims, citations, and evidence strength
component_proofs.expected_output: a structured appraisal view of what the source actually says
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (candidate notes, extracted claims, citations...) produces output (a structured appraisal view of what the source...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to judge methodological strength and evidentiary credibility
component_proofs.input: study design, methods notes, and quality indicators
component_proofs.expected_output: strength, weakness, and reliability judgments
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (study design, methods notes, and quality indicators) produces output (strength, weakness, and reliability judgments) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to clarify how a source works analytically or transferably
component_proofs.input: claims, procedures, causal logic, and transfer hypotheses
component_proofs.expected_output: explicit mechanism notes and role definitions
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (claims, procedures, causal logic, and transfer...) produces output (explicit mechanism notes and role definitions) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: multiple candidates compete for the same analytical function
component_proofs.input: side-by-side candidate records and evaluation criteria

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (side-by-side candidate records and evaluation criteria) produces output (ranked comparisons and preferred-source choices) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to apply the same appraisal criteria across a larger review set

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (candidate set, appraisal rules, and target thresholds) produces output (consistent first-pass source decisions at scale) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to understand how and why earlier judgments changed

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (decision history, revisions, and analyst notes) produces output (traceable judgment lineage) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you want the distribution of advance, hold, and exclude decisions across the current review set

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (candidate mappings, source structures, and target...) produces output (depth judgments on the proposed mapping) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: several alignments are plausible and one must be selected or rejected

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (approved mappings and mechanism notes) produces output (candidate transfer hypotheses and comparative...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (mapping structure, scope, mechanism clarity...) produces output (quality scores and mapping weaknesses) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to compare a new mapping against prior successful structures
component_proofs.input: stored mappings, prior notes, and reusable analogical patterns
component_proofs.expected_output: retrieved precedents and reusable mapping templates
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (stored mappings, prior notes, and reusable analogical...) produces output (retrieved precedents and reusable mapping templates) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (field maturity, chronology, and development-stage...) produces output (timing compatibility judgments) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (competing findings, conflicting premises, and draft...) produces output (a tension register showing what must be reconciled...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need a summary view of whether the room is producing ideas that are both fresh and disciplined

component_proofs.expected_output: a room-level balance between novelty, coherence, and refinement
component_proofs.verification_check: Test one representative case; confirm input (active insight portfolio, maturity signals...) produces output (a room-level balance between novelty, coherence...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to verify that a draft still points back to the findings and mappings that justify it

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to see whether edits sharpened the argument or only changed its tone

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to estimate what kind of scholarly contribution the draft plausibly makes

component_proofs.claim_supported: you need to prepare one or more drafts for export or handoff

minimum_public_demo: show one initial research question entering Observatory/Galaxy, one Bridge mapping, one XPol pressure test, and one Forge-ready output with stale-state rules visible
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
